creator: ek@purplehat.org
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

Hi Patrick,

	Thanks for submitting this. It has actually been a burden with the
Maia port for a very long time. It's a very strange issue that only
seems to effect certain systems. I'm wondering if perhaps it is
related to the way that MySQL is built or perhaps the system arch
setting itself. I've been trying to resolve this issue for over a year
and I cannot reproduce it myself on any of my i386 or amd64 systems.
So, this was a big help identifying the fact that the "ldconfig" check
seems to be the culprit. I will look into this with the help of some
FBSD port gurus and see what I can do. I'll post back as soon as I
have a definitive way of fixing this. Again, thanks for submitting
this! Without being able to reproduce the error, there is really no
way for me to figuring this out!

Regards,
Janky Jay

On 04/03/2013 02:13 PM, Edwin Groothuis wrote:
> Maintainer of security/maia,
> 
> Please note that PR ports/177610 has just been submitted.
> 
> If it contains a patch for an upgrade, an enhancement or a bug fix 
> you agree on, reply to this email stating that you approve the
> patch and a committer will take care of it.
> 
> The full text of the PR can be found at: 
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/177610
>
